title: Ilex tetramera
intro: Ilex tetramera, commonly known as the Chinese holly or Japanese holly, is a species of holly in the family Aquifoliaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in protecting against oxidative stress, reducing inflammation, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Ilex tetramera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Ilex tetramera is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, as well as being used in capsule or tablet form, to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
taxonomy: Ilex tetramera is a species within the genus Ilex, which is a group of evergreen shrubs and small trees commonly known as holly. The genus Ilex is part of the family Aquifoliaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. This family is further classified within the order Aquifoliales, which is a distinct order of flowering plants. The class of flowering plants to which Ilex tetramera belongs is Magnoliopsida, and the phylum is Angiospermophyta. Ilex tetramera's classification also includes the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Ilex tetramera is a plant species that is traditionally used for medicinal purposes in various cultures. The leaves and berries of the plant are often used to treat a range of health issues. Traditionally, the plant is used to treat fever, digestive problems, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Ilex tetramera for medicinal purposes. Its potential medicinal properties and uses are still being studied and documented.

constituents: Ilex tetramera, also known as the Queensland Holly, contains a range of active constituents, including iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and terpenoids. These compounds contribute to its potential medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The specific iridoid glycosides present in Ilex tetramera have been identified as aucubin and 7-acetoxylongistachyoside. Additionally, the plant's flavonoids and terpenoids may play a role in its antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of Ilex tetramera's active constituents and their potential applications.

preparations: Ilex tetramera, also known as the four-angled holly, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its purported health benefits.
